title: GDPR Records of Processing Activities Completeness

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: CIM Network_Traffic and Authentication data models, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` (processing activities register), ES Asset Framework
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(Authentication.user) as users, count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.dest
| rename Authentication.dest as dest
| lookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv system_name AS dest OUTPUT processing_activity, data_category, legal_basis, retention_period, last_review_date
| eval in_register=if(isnotnull(processing_activity), "Yes", "NOT_IN_ROPA")
| eval review_overdue=if(isnotnull(last_review_date) AND (now()-strptime(last_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400 > 365, "OVERDUE", "OK")
| where in_register="NOT_IN_ROPA" OR review_overdue="OVERDUE"
| sort - users
| table dest, users, count, in_register, processing_activity, legal_basis, review_overdue
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
description: Article 30 requires controllers to maintain documented records of processing activities including purposes, data categories, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and technical measures. This use case validates that the processing register (ROPA) is complete and current by cross-referencing it against observed data flows and systems, surfacing systems that process personal data but are not in the register ‚Äî a gap regulators frequently cite during inspections.

This use case validates that the processing register (ROPA) is complete and current by cross-referencing it against observed data flows and systems, surfacing systems that process personal data but are not in the register ‚Äî a gap regulators frequently cite during inspections.
grandmaExplanation: Requires controllers to maintain documented records of processing activities including purposes, data categories, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and technical measures ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Maintain `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` from your DPO's Article 30 register (exported from OneTrust, DataGrail, or manual spreadsheet); (2) compare active systems receiving authentication events against registered systems; (3) alert on systems with user activity not in the ROPA; (4) flag entries with reviews older than 12 months; (5) schedule quarterly for ROPA completeness auditing.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: CIM Network_Traffic and Authentication data models, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` (processing activities register), ES Asset Framework.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` from your DPO's Article 30 register (exported from OneTrust, DataGrail, or manual spreadsheet); (2) compare active systems receiving authentication events against registered systems; (3) alert on systems with user activity not in the ROPA; (4) flag entries with reviews older than 12 months; (5) schedule quarterly for ROPA completeness auditing.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(Authentication.user) as users, count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.dest
| rename Authentication.dest as dest
| lookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv system_name AS dest OUTPUT processing_activity, data_category, legal_basis, retention_period, last_review_date
| eval in_register=if(isnotnull(processing_activity), "Yes", "NOT_IN_ROPA")
| eval review_overdue=if(isnotnull(last_review_date) AND (now()-strptime(last_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400 > 365, "OVERDUE", "OK")
| where in_register="NOT_IN_ROPA" OR review_overdue="OVERDUE"
| sort - users
| table dest, users, count, in_register, processing_activity, legal_basis, review_overdue
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Records of Processing Activities Completeness (Art. 30)** ‚Äî Article 30 requires controllers to maintain documented records of processing activities including purposes, data categories, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and technical measures. This use case validates that the processing register (ROPA) is complete and current by cross-referencing it against observed data flows and systems, surfacing systems that process personal data but are not in the register ‚Äî a gap regulators frequently cite during inspections.

Documented **Data sources**: CIM Network_Traffic and Authentication data models, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` (processing activities register), ES Asset Framework. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **in_register** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **review_overdue** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where in_register="NOT_IN_ROPA" OR review_overdue="OVERDUE"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **GDPR Records of Processing Activities Completeness (Art. 30)**): table dest, users, count, in_register, processing_activity, legal_basis, review_overdue

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**GDPR Records of Processing Activities Completeness (Art. 30)** ‚Äî Article 30 requires controllers to maintain documented records of processing activities including purposes, data categories, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and technical measures. This use case validates that the processing register (ROPA) is complete and current by cross-referencing it against observed data flows and systems, surfacing systems that process personal data but are not in the register ‚Äî a gap regulators frequently cite during inspections.

Documented **Data sources**: CIM Network_Traffic and Authentication data models, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv` (processing activities register), ES Asset Framework. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unregistered systems), Single value (ROPA coverage %), Bar chart (users by unregistered system), Timeline (review dates).
visualization: Table (unregistered systems), Single value (ROPA coverage %), Bar chart (users by unregistered system), Timeline (review dates).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
